title: UMI Image Layered World Model
description: UMI Image Layered World Model: build tri-view robot video world modeling, decompose videos into robot / object-contact / scene layers, and prove layered data improves manipulation.
summary: UMI Image Layered World Model is now organized around three execution lanes: Stage 1 Wan2.1 dual-wrist / tri-view shared-context world modeling, using Gamma-World, COMBO, and Solaris as multi-agent / multi-view references plus exact prior baselines (Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers and nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID); Stage 2 video layer decomposition/completion with masks, VACE, RoboTwin, MaskWAM, SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour SVG sidecars and inpaint baselines; and Stage 3 policy/pose utility tests proving that layered data helps IDM, FoundationPose, rewards, background replacement, and downstream policies.

subprojects.label: A
subprojects.title: UMI Ego World
subprojects.body: Generate synchronized action-conditioned robot videos from wrist + robot-head ego views.

subprojects.label: B
subprojects.title: Robot Video Layers
subprojects.body: Decompose real or generated robot videos into recomposable robot / object-contact / occluder / scene RGBA layers with inpaint-behind-occlusion, recomposition QA, and SVG/contact sidecars. v0 route locked 2026-06-21: MaskWAM-style VLM+SAM3 annotation loop -> per-entity matting -> VACE masked-V2V occluder/scene fill -> recomposition QA; RoboTwin 2.0 synthetic masks as the GT spine; Qwen-Image-Layered and RevealLayer dropped from the mainline after group testing.
subprojects.output: recomposable RGBA layer stack + masks/alpha + optional SVG sidecar + pose + recomposition-QA scores

subprojects.label: C
subprojects.title: Layered Data Utility
subprojects.body: Use layers for IDM / pose / VO, process reward, RL supervision, and scene adaptation.

asset_alt: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan architecture diagram for UMI, showing left and right gripper camera histories, shared video VAE/tokenizer, condition encoder, view embeddings, shared Wan backbone, dual prediction heads, and training objectives.
asset_caption: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan: UMI Stage 1 architecture for separate left/right wrist video latents, view embeddings, shared Wan2.1 backbone, dual decoded outputs, and cross-view / temporal / state-action consistency losses.
details: Sub-project A, UMI Ego World for robot-data generation: train or adapt an action-conditioned multi-view world model that generates synchronized robot videos from UMI-style wrist views plus a fixed or floating robot-head/main view. The Stage 1 requirement is shared-context multi-video generation: left/right wrist and head/fixed outputs must describe the same scene, object state, contacts, and action consequences in their overlapping fields of view rather than drifting as independent videos.

Stage 1 / Sub-project A reference update 2026-06-19: treat Gamma-World, COMBO, and Solaris as the reference set for multi-agent / multi-view shared-world modeling. Gamma-World contributes permutation-symmetric agent/view identity, sparse hub-style cross-stream context exchange, and causal real-time rollout; COMBO contributes partial egocentric observations -> latent/global world state, compositional joint-action conditioning, and planning/evaluation around other agents' intent; Solaris contributes synchronized multiplayer video+action capture, staged single-view -> multi-view training, Self Forcing / Checkpointed Self Forcing for long-horizon stability, and explicit view-consistency evaluation. For UMI, map players/agents to wrist/head camera streams and robot hands: the novelty is not generic multi-player video, but robot-data utility, overlap consistency, view/layer contracts, IDM/control decoding, and policy/reward ablations.

Dataset decision 2026-06-17: Hoi! is the selected tri-view / multi ego-view replacement substrate; keep the public download page in the project intro: https://timengelbracht.github.io/Hoi-Dataset-Website/#download. It provides synchronized `umi` and `wrist` capture bundles with head-ego, manipulator-centric ego, and fixed external views; MV-UMI and EgoMimic stay as backup/reference datasets, while raw file mirroring and model-eval smoke runs are downstream execution items rather than blockers for the replacement decision.

Sub-project B, Robot Video Image-Layered Model: build a general layer decomposition model for robot videos, not just a one-off image editing tool. Inputs can be real UMI clips or generated world-model rollouts; outputs should be temporally consistent robot layer, object/contact layer, background/scene layer, occluder/human masks, recomposition QA, and optional SVG/structured layer metadata.

Stage 2 / Sub-project B update 2026-06-18: RevealLayer (arXiv:2605.11818) is now demoted from short-term build-upon route to paper/reference only. Its occlusion-aware RGBA formulation remains intellectually relevant, but current robot-scene tests did not produce usable decomposition, so the practical pipeline should not depend on RevealLayer-style direct image-to-layer generation.

Layered Image Policy sync 2026-06-16: the team ran a real robot-bed still-image probe and a short video-segmentation replay. Current qualitative read: GPT-generated split layers plus PSD assembly were stronger than the first RevealLayer / Qwen-style sample, but recomposition still showed visible alignment and missing-pixel issues; a SAM3-style video segmentation demo showed stable tracked robot masks across frames, suggesting the near-term practical route is mask tracking plus iterative segment+inpaint for video.

Layered Image Policy sync 2026-06-17/18: Meng Ziyang shared SAM3 segmentation plus GPT inpaint outputs, and the group judged the result qualitatively strong; this keeps mask tracking + GPT/inpaint background completion as the strongest immediate practical route for video-ready layer data. Yongxi's Qwen Image-Layered comparison exposed a decisive failure mode: Qwen can specify layer count but cannot bind semantics to each layer; prompts act more like captions, so it merges visually or physically connected regions, creates residual/occlusion/shadow/support layers, fails pure-background rendering, and weakly completes pillow/background regions. The shared Qwen failure gallery is saved as dashboard/assets/umi-qwen-layer-failure-20260618-{0,1,2,3}.jpg. Decision: pass/drop Qwen Image-Layered and RevealLayer/ICML-style outputs as short-term routes; continue with SAM3/SAM mask tracking, GPT/inpaint or VACE, RoboTwin/MaskWAM mask audits, and SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour SVG sidecars, with StarVector/OmniSVG as optional still-frame probes and SAMVG retained only as a paper/reference until code appears.

MaskWAM pipeline import 2026-06-21: the useful part for our global layered dataset plan is not its single-mask policy format, but its annotation loop: language annotation -> VLM task-object extraction -> SAM3 text/point-conditioned mask propagation -> human verification -> labeled episodes. Use this as the v0 data-prep scaffold for robot/object/contact/occluder/background pseudo-labels: initialize masks from task-relevant prompts, ask humans only on ambiguous objects or bad propagation rounds, and record the verification decision in layer_manifest.json. The dashboard asset dashboard/assets/maskwam-annotation-pipeline-20260621.png and weekly memo backup weekly-briefs/memos/maskwam-vs-umi-image-layered-20260621.md capture the comparison.

Sub-project C, Layered Robot Data for low-level control: test how each layer is consumed by downstream robot learning. Robot layer -> IDM and pose estimation; wrist robot/camera layer -> visual odometry / SLAM; object/contact layer -> process reward or RL self-supervision; background layer -> domain randomization and scene adaptation.

Robot-layer route: for whole-arm visible views, use Dr. Robot / differentiable robot rendering as a reference for reconstructing robot pose or joint state from visual appearance; for simpler gripper-only settings, estimate gripper pose directly and use the visual transition as inverse-dynamics supervision.

Wrist-view geometry route: when the robot is not fully visible, use ORB-SLAM3-style visual or visual-inertial SLAM, feed-forward geometry, and camera egomotion to recover the wrist/head motion context, then let IDM infer actions from object and scene transitions rather than arm pixels alone.

Object-layer route: treat object state, contact, and task progress as the reward-bearing dynamics. LIV-style video/language reward models and object-centric trajectory methods become the reference for process reward, failure attribution, and RL fine-tuning signals.

Background-layer route: keep object and robot evidence fixed while varying scene/background for robustness. RoboEngine-style semantic robot segmentation plus background generation is the immediate analogy; the layer model should make this cleaner than flat-image augmentation.

Prior evidence: ARRO shows that SAM/open-vocabulary segmentation and task-specific visual masking can improve robot policies. Our stronger claim is that a full Image-Layered Robot Data representation, beyond masks alone, should improve manipulation by separating robot, object/contact, and scene factors.

Dashboard asset update 2026-06-15: the world-model taxonomy figure is retained as a reference asset rather than the current project visual. Use it as a reminder that DINO/MAE/NeRF/scene-flow/video-diffusion/Dreamer/MuJoCo-style claims are not the same capability. Tri-View should state whether a component reconstructs state, predicts next observations, or supports executable rollouts. Current visual was superseded on 2026-06-17 by the UMI Model Structure screenshot.

Main experiment framing: compare flat RGB robot data vs SAM/mask-only data vs Image-Layered Robot Data on the same manipulation policy/eval tasks. Success requires gains in policy robustness, IDM recoverability, reward/eval quality, or domain adaptation, not only visually clean layer images.

Stage separation: Stage 1 builds on Gamma-World-style synchronized action-conditioned world modeling; Stage 2 now prioritizes mask-tracked video layer decomposition plus inpaint/completion and SVG/contact sidecars rather than direct Qwen/RevealLayer image-to-layer generation. The connecting claim is that generated or real UMI videos become more useful once they are decomposed into robot, object/contact, occluder, and background layers for IDM, reward/eval, and policy ablations.

SVG/layer pilot update 2026-06-19: a one-frame MolmoAct robot-couch pseudo-label package in ~/Downloads/molmoact_svg_layer_pilot_20260617 defines robot_visible, object_contact, and scene_background mask/RGBA/SVG outputs, layer_manifest.json, recomposition/overlay previews, QA metrics, and failure notes. WeChat review of UMI World Model and Layered Image Policy confirmed prior tests of GPT split PSD, RevealLayer, SAM3 + GPT inpaint, and Qwen Image-Layered, but no SVG-vectorization implementation run. Yongxi follow-up on 2026-06-18/19 corrected the implementation route: SAMVG has no open-source code; SuperSVG is the SAMVG replacement, SuperSVG + LIVE are the active SVG 化验证 baselines, StarVector / OmniSVG are candidate probes, and simple SAM-mask-to-contour remains the practical baseline. Decision: SVG is useful as a structured contour/contact metadata sidecar, while raster RGBA/masks/video remain the training truth. The next active item is a SuperSVG/LIVE SVG layer probe, not another generic segment+inpaint comparison.

Global layered dataset gate update 2026-06-21: v0 should select a pragmatic mask-first, layer-later pipeline. Candidate matrix now compares SAM3+GPT inpaint, VACE/VAC/VAS video inpaint, RoboTwin synthetic masks, MaskWAM-style SAM3/VLM/human verification, and SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour sidecars, while treating Qwen Image-Layered and RevealLayer as negative/reference baselines. The decision criterion is not visual aesthetics; it is whether the route yields named layer outputs, mask/RGBA/video/SVG schema, speed/cost estimates, human verification load, temporal consistency, recomposition QA, and measurable FoundationPose/IDM/reward utility on the next episode batch.

UMI model structure update 2026-06-17: Stage 1 now has two explicit routes. Route A is direct left/right wrist-camera frame stitching followed by Wan2.1 finetuning; its data prep is text annotation plus video stitching, with 10Kh RealOmni-Open Dataset as the immediate external data source. Route B is the stronger shared-backbone dual-branch Wan: encode left and right wrist-camera histories into separate video latents, add view embeddings to distinguish cameras, fuse robot proprioception/state, future action sequence, camera pose/calibration, and optional task text, then train a shared Wan2.1 backbone with dual prediction heads / shared VAE decoder for left and right future videos.

Shared-backbone dual-branch Wan objective update 2026-06-17: training objectives should include diffusion/reconstruction loss for predicted latents, cross-view consistency loss between left/right predictions, temporal consistency loss, and optional state/action consistency loss that aligns predicted video dynamics with robot state and future actions. This is the Stage 1 model-architecture complement to the existing Gamma-World baseline, not a replacement for the later Image-Layered/SVG decomposition track.

Wan2.1 vs Wan2.2 training decision 2026-06-17: use Wan2.1 for the first UMI dual-wrist training route. Reason: Wan2.2-A14B is a two-expert MoE denoiser with high-noise and low-noise experts split by SNR/timestep; adapting it would require separate high/low expert LoRA or finetune runs, expert-boundary selection, paired inference composition, and extra failure analysis when one expert learns but the other does not. Wan2.1 keeps a single dense denoiser/backbone and has mature LoRA/full-finetune recipes in VideoX-Fun, DiffSynth-Studio, finetrainers, and diffusion-pipe. Keep Wan2.2 for later quality comparison or TI2V-5B exploration after the Wan2.1 concat baseline and dual-latent schema are verified.

Wan2.2-A14B action/state probe 2026-06-19: on the 8xH20 AutoDL node, DiffSynth-Studio was extended with latent-time-aligned UMI condition tokens, a zero-init residual control encoder, a dual-view adapter, and a memory-efficient wrong-condition contrastive backward path for the two Wan2.2-A14B experts. Full 49-frame 320x768 row5 overfit completed for four high/low LoRA pairs. Best current recipe is roll negative with weight 0.5 / margin 0.02: actual MAE 46.83, while time-view-only, zero-condition, and wrong-row0 conditions are worse by +4.01, +4.42, and +3.34 MAE. This proves action/state conditioning is no longer ignored as completely as the earlier broadcast/gated probes, but visual differences remain moderate; next step should be a stronger ControlNet-style residual branch plus action/state consistency head rather than only more adapter sweeps. Local report: ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

Wan2.2-A14B block-control update 2026-06-19: implemented a zero-init UMIBlockControlBranch for action/state control. The x-dependent branch passed 1-step full-res smoke but OOMed during 30-step 49-frame 320x768 training because each selected DiT block had to retain large 5120-d hidden activations. The practical route is the no-x block-control branch: pre-project action/state once into a bottleneck control tensor and inject it block-wise. Four high/low expert pairs were trained in parallel on 8 H20 GPUs. Best recipe is nox_roll_w05_m002: actual MAE 43.50 / PSNR 11.80, with time-view-only +8.56, zero-condition +8.62, and wrong-row0 +6.10 MAE gaps. This beats the previous full-res contrastive baseline (46.83 MAE, wrong-row0 +3.34). No-contrast reconstructs but ignores action/state, confirming contrastive pressure is necessary. Next baseline: keep nox_roll_w05_m002, add lightweight state/action consistency head, then implement clean shared-backbone dual-branch Wan with explicit left/right outputs and cross-view consistency.

Shared dual-branch B0 2026-06-19: added a `shared_lr` metadata/validator path so left and right wrist clips are separate branch samples trained by one shared LoRA/backbone and sampled independently with `--view left/right --lora-view shared_lr`. Row5 high/low Wan2.2-A14B overfit completed: step-1 smoke succeeded and step-60 checkpoints produced. Step-60 validation: left actual MAE 68.52 / PSNR 9.01, right actual MAE 66.99 / PSNR 8.60, mean actual MAE 67.76; wrong-row0 mean gap is only +2.30. Conclusion: the shared-backbone/separate-output plumbing is verified, but simple left/right sample mixing is much weaker than the packed dual_lr no-x block-control baseline (MAE 43.50, wrong-row0 +6.10). The next real architecture step is simultaneous paired latent batching with view embeddings, explicit cross-view residual/attention between paired branch tokens, separate left/right outputs, and reconstruction + action/state + cross-view consistency losses.

Paired dual-branch adapter update 2026-06-19: implemented the first true simultaneous paired-latent route. Each metadata row now carries left/right wrist videos and left/right UMI action-state tensors; the training module encodes both branches, concatenates them as batch=2, adds batch-view embeddings, and applies a zero-init paired cross-view residual after Wan DiT blocks. Smoke step-1 passed, then four high/low expert pairs trained in parallel on 8 H20 GPUs for 30-step row5 overfit. Best recipe is paired_nox_roll_w05_m002: actual mean MAE 55.33 / PSNR 10.36, with time-view-only and zero-condition both 60.61 (+5.28), and wrong-row0 59.25 (+3.92). This improves strongly over shared_lr B0 (67.76) and proves real paired forward + action/state sensitivity, but it still trails the packed dual_lr no-x baseline (43.50). Next step is a stronger paired attention/control branch rather than more metadata-only sweeps.

Paired cross-view consistency sweep 2026-06-19: added an explicit temporal-profile cross-view consistency loss and trained four high/low Wan2.2-A14B paired routes in parallel on 8 H20 GPUs for row5 step-30. Best weight was xview_w005: actual mean MAE 65.17 / PSNR 8.99, with zero and time-view-only both 68.23 (+3.06) and wrong-row0 65.76 (+0.59). Other weights were worse: w002 MAE 73.37, w010 MAE 69.68, w020 MAE 71.50. Conclusion: this scalar consistency term over-regularizes and weakens action/state dependence, so reject it as the next mainline. Keep packed nox_roll_w05_m002 as the quantitative baseline and paired_nox_roll_w05_m002 as the best true paired architecture baseline. Local report updated at ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

Paired control-mix sweep 2026-06-19: tested a stronger paired adapter variant where same-index left/right residual mixing consumes the UMI block-control tensor directly. The code path is now guarded by an explicit `umi_paired_dual_branch_use_control` switch so old paired baselines are not silently changed. Four high/low expert pairs trained on 8 H20 GPUs. Best recipe was paired_ctrlmix_roll_w05_m002: actual mean MAE 58.55 / PSNR 9.78, zero and time-view-only 61.06 (+2.51), wrong-row0 59.63 (+1.09). Pair scale 0.10 regressed to 61.67, margin 0.05 to 63.50, and contrast weight 1.0 collapsed to 73.37. Conclusion: simple same-index control-mix is also weaker than paired_nox_roll_w05_m002 (55.33, wrong-row0 +3.92). Next architecture step should be true paired attention or native dual-output heads, not scalar profile loss or token-wise residual mixing.

Frame-pooled paired attention sweep 2026-06-19: implemented a true paired-attention adapter that pools each left/right branch per latent frame, attends to the opposite branch as K/V, and broadcasts a zero-init per-frame residual back into the shared Wan2.2-A14B backbone. Remote tensor test and high/low step-1 smoke passed; 8-GPU row5 step-30 sweep completed. Best attention recipe was paired_attn512_roll_w05_m002: actual mean MAE 61.79 / PSNR 9.58, zero and time-view-only 64.65 (+2.86), wrong-row0 62.67 (+0.88). attn256 was 69.41, attn256+control 67.01, and attn256 scale0.10 71.77. Conclusion: frame-pooled paired attention is a real paired-attention seam but still regresses versus paired_nox_roll_w05_m002 (55.33, wrong-row0 +3.92). Next architecture step should move from post-block adapters to native dual-output training or loss masking inside one forward pass.

Branch-masked control loss sweep 2026-06-19: implemented branch-specific wrong-condition negatives for the packed dual_lr Wan2.2-A14B route. The loss can corrupt only left or right UMI condition tokens and apply the contrastive hinge only to the corresponding spatial half of the generated canvas; validators now include left_zero/right_zero plus per-half MAE. All four high/low expert pairs completed on the H20 server. Best quality is nox_branchonly_w025_m002: actual MAE 46.11 / PSNR 10.90, zero +4.46, time-view-only +2.97, wrong-row0 +2.14. Best branch-separation diagnostic is nox_branchonly_w05_m002: actual MAE 59.54, zero +7.53, time-view-only +6.01, wrong-row0 +7.89, with left_zero mainly hurting the left half and right_zero mainly hurting the right half. Full+branch contrastive at 0.5 collapses to MAE 80.17, so reject heavy combined contrastive. Keep nox_roll_w05_m002 as the best packed baseline (43.50), keep branchonly_w025 as the quality-preserving branch-mask recipe, and use branchonly_w05 as the control-sensitivity diagnostic. Local report updated at ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

AutoDL compute constraint 2026-06-17: AutoDL should be treated as single-machine training only for this project. It does not support cross-machine distributed training in our planned setup, so the practical ceiling is one 8-GPU node. Wan2.1 Route A/B configs must fit within single-node 8-card training: prefer LoRA or small full-finetune trials first, use gradient accumulation / checkpointing / offload as needed, and avoid plans that require multi-node DeepSpeed/FSDP. This further supports starting with Wan2.1 rather than Wan2.2 high/low expert finetuning.

VACE video inpainting probe 2026-06-17: add Wan2.1 VACE as the main video-inpainting / masked video-to-video candidate for Stage 2 layer completion. Placement: after SAM/SAM2/SAM3 mask tracking and before recomposition QA, use VACE to fill robot/object/occluder-removed regions or complete background clips. VACE should be compared against GPT/FLUX-style still/image-agent inpaint and existing segment+inpaint baselines; RevealLayer/Qwen are now negative direct-layer references, not active short-term raster-completion routes. SVG remains mask/contour/contact metadata sidecar, while VACE owns temporally coherent raster inpaint/background completion.

Project header image update 2026-06-17: replaced the earlier cropped UMI Model Structure screenshot with the clean Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan diagram as the project head image. Use it as the visual anchor for the Wan2.1 dual-wrist route: left/right gripper histories -> shared VAE/tokenizer -> view/time/position-conditioned fusion -> shared Wan backbone -> dual left/right video outputs.

Layered Image Policy mask-data update 2026-06-17: group chat added two Stage 2 TODOs. First, audit RoboTwin/RoboTwin 2.0 data because its SAPIEN simulation can provide grayscale object masks with per-object separation; confirm whether those masks can seed robot/object/contact/occluder/background pseudo-layer labels. Second, compare MaskWAM's mask prompting + future mask prediction pipeline against our Image-Layered Robot Data route. MaskWAM is a mask-centric WAM; our route is layer decomposition: RGBA/video layers, inpainted background, SVG mask/contour sidecars, and downstream IDM/reward/recomposition QA.

MaskWAM differentiation note 2026-06-17: MaskWAM segments or consumes masks as spatial prompts and predicts future masks as object-centric supervision. That is adjacent but not identical to UMI Image-Layered data. Our proposed contribution should be framed as decomposing robot videos into named, editable, recomposable layers: robot, object/contact, occluder, scene/background, plus inpainted raster completion and SVG/contact metadata. The comparison should borrow MaskWAM's mask-as-prompt and future-mask-prediction ideas where useful, but emphasize that we evaluate layer purity, recomposition, temporal consistency, IDM/reward utility, and background replacement rather than mask prediction alone.

UMI meeting sync 2026-06-17: project framing was restated as three connected subprojects: (1) learn/share the overlap and correspondence between left/right wrist views, (2) turn robot videos into layer-decomposed data with robot/action, object/contact, occluder, and scene/background layers, and (3) test policy/pose/reward utility on those layers. Haoyu owns Wan2.1 dual-wrist data/training and the shared-backbone dual-branch architecture; Ziyang owns SAM3/video segmentation, video inpainting/completion, and FoundationPose utility probes; Lai owns multi-layer count and inference deployment tests; Boris owns dashboard synchronization, nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID / Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers / RealOmni follow-up, and GPU-resource escalation.

Layering method selection gate 2026-06-18: by next week or the week after, the team should choose the first practical layered-data pipeline and start preparing the global layered dataset build. Qwen Image-Layered and RevealLayer/ICML-style direct layer generation have been passed over for the current robot scene. The live comparison set should now focus on GPT/SAM3 inpaint, VACE/VAC/VAS-style video inpainting, RoboTwin synthetic masks, MaskWAM-style mask prompting/prediction, and SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour SVG sidecars, with StarVector/OmniSVG as optional SVG probes. The gate is not only visual quality: it must check temporal consistency, masked-region completion, unmasked preservation, layer purity, recomposition QA, FoundationPose/IDM/reward utility, speed/cost, and whether human layer annotation is needed.

FoundationPose utility note 2026-06-17: Ziyang should test whether feeding layered outputs improves object pose extraction. Compare raw RGB, object-only layer, object+background layer, and recomposed layered clips; report success/failure cases, occlusion handling, and whether object/contact layers help downstream pose and reward/eval heads.

Model-name correction 2026-06-17: prior WAN baseline reports should spell out Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers (5B) rather than generic Wan2.2, and Cosmos reports should spell out nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID rather than generic Cosmos3. Some downloaded artifacts keep local path labels such as Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Turbo-Diffusers; the dashboard-facing model identity should still preserve the exact 5B WAN and nvidia Cosmos checkpoint names.

Paired branch-specific contrastive loss 2026-06-19: implemented branch-specific wrong-condition negatives for the true paired-batch Wan2.2-A14B path, where left/right wrist clips are separate batch items rather than spatial halves of a packed canvas. Remote tensor tests, high/low step-1 smoke, two high/low 30-step row5 overfit runs, and 12 validation jobs completed on the H20 server. Result is a clear negative: paired_branchonly_w025_m002 actual mean MAE 66.33 / PSNR 9.09, while zero 61.95 (-4.38) and wrongrow0 64.14 (-2.20) are better than actual; paired_branchonly_w05_m002 collapses to actual MAE 75.55, with zero 63.39 (-12.15) and wrongrow0 65.77 (-9.77). This rejects branch-specific contrastive loss alone as the path to controllable dual-view generation. Keep packed nox_roll_w05_m002 as the best quantitative baseline (43.50 MAE), keep packed branchonly_w025/w05 as diagnostics, and move the next implementation toward native dual-output/control-branch training: branch-specific condition injection before or inside attention, optional ControlNet-style zero-init residual branch, and dual-head/dual-loss training in one forward pass. Local report updated at ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

Pre-block paired control injection 2026-06-19: implemented UMIPreBlockControlInjector and wired it into DiffSynth Wan before each DiT block so action/state residuals can affect attention rather than only post-block corrections. Remote py_compile, bash checks, tensor no-op/backward test, high/low step-1 smoke, two high/low 30-step row5 overfit runs, and 8 validation jobs completed. Preblock-only is rejected: paired_preblock_w05_m002 actual MAE 68.24 / PSNR 8.93, zero 68.01 (-0.24), wrongrow0 67.10 (-1.14). Preblock+nox is usable but not leading: paired_preblock_nox_w05_m002 actual MAE 60.46 / PSNR 9.59, zero 66.54 (+6.08), time-view-only 66.54 (+6.08), wrongrow0 63.68 (+3.22). This proves early action/state injection can create condition sensitivity, but reconstruction still trails paired_nox_roll_w05_m002 (55.33) and packed nox_roll_w05_m002 (43.50). Next step should be true in-block branch-specific condition adapters or native dual-output heads, not more post-block residual stacking. Local report updated at ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

Paired block-modulation adapter update 2026-06-19: implemented UMIBlockModulationAdapter for Wan2.2-A14B paired training. The adapter predicts zero-init per-block deltas for Wan DiT AdaLN shift/scale/gate streams, so action/state can enter inside each block rather than only as pre/post-block residuals. Local and remote py_compile/bash checks passed; remote tensor no-op/backward test covered both t_mod [B,6,D] and [B,N,6,D]; high/low step-1 smoke passed; two high/low 30-step row5 paired overfit runs completed on the H20 server. Blockmod-only is rejected: paired_blockmod_w05_m002 actual MAE 70.13 / PSNR 8.38, zero/time 71.04 (+0.91), wrongrow0 67.67 (-2.45). Blockmod+nox is usable but not leading: paired_blockmod_nox_w05_m002 actual MAE 62.06 / PSNR 9.51, zero/time 69.43 (+7.37), wrongrow0 64.41 (+2.34). This proves in-block modulation can carry action/state signal when paired with no-x control, but reconstruction still trails preblock+nox (60.46), paired_nox_roll_w05_m002 (55.33), and packed nox_roll_w05_m002 (43.50). Next step should combine paired batch semantics with native dual-output/control-branch training rather than stacking more residual adapters. Local report updated at ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html.

Paired output-head native dual-output probe 2026-06-19: implemented and validated a first native paired output-head route for Wan2.2-A14B. Left and right wrist videos are generated as separate outputs rather than a packed canvas, with action/state carried through latent-time-aligned condition tokens and paired no-x control. Row5 step-30 validation completed on the H20 server for actual, zero-condition, time-view-only, and wrong-row0 conditions. Result: actual mean MAE 54.41 / PSNR 10.14; zero and time-view-only both 60.20 (+5.79), and wrong-row0 58.97 (+4.55). The left branch carries most of the condition benefit (actual left 63.78 vs zero 73.33), while the right branch changes only weakly (45.05 vs 47.08). Conclusion: native output heads plus aligned action/state tokens are a positive but still weak control signal; do not spend the next cycle on more token-only sweeps. Next implementation should move to a zero-init residual ControlNet-style branch or native in-block branch-specific action/state control head, while keeping packed nox_roll_w05_m002 (43.50 MAE) as the quantitative baseline. Local report section and gallery: ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html#paired-output-head-20260619.

Paired output-space residual head validation 2026-06-20: high/low Wan2.2-A14B row5 step-30 training completed after the Gamma-World/Cosmos2.5 job freed the H20 GPUs. The UMIPairedOutputResidualHead path is a weak/negative result: actual mean MAE 68.85 / PSNR 8.92, zero and time_view_only both 69.87 (+1.02 MAE), and wrongrow0 67.17 (-1.67 MAE, better than actual). This means adding a zero-init residual after dit.head in patch-latent output space does not create reliable action/state control; it mostly reconstructs from visual context and can be improved by unrelated condition tokens. Reject output-space residual stacking as the next mainline. Keep paired_output_head as the better native-output probe (54.41 MAE), keep packed nox_roll_w05_m002 as the quantitative baseline (43.50 MAE), and move the next attempt to pre-/in-block branch-specific action/state control plus explicit state/action consistency. Local report/gallery updated: ~/Downloads/umi_dual_view_adapter_20260619/index.html#paired-output-residual-head-20260620.

Multi-row paired output-residual strict gate 2026-06-21: trained four high/low Wan2.2-A14B paired-output residual variants on the 8xH20 node to step-64, then ran rows 0/1/5/7/11 with actual, zero, time_view_only, and wrong-row conditions. The strict action gate improved to 4/20 passes, compared with 0/20 for the previous cross-row blockmod gate and 3/20 for the older actioncontrast best. Best current recipe is paired_outres_nox_gc1_m0p1_h768_b512_scale0p05_r4_multiclip_e4 with 2/5 passes: row5 actual MAE 74.43 with deltas +1.78 vs zero/time and +2.48 vs wrongrow0; row7 actual MAE 70.45 with deltas +4.46 vs zero/time and +1.28 vs wrongrow0. h512_scale0p05 gets 1/5, xview_scale0p05 gets 1/5, and scale0p08 gets 0/5. A follow-up high-action/high-visible-change gate on rows 14/15/9/6/2 for this best recipe gets 1/5: row2 passes with +2.54 vs zero/time and +2.47 vs wrongrow0, row15 is close but below margin, and rows 6/9/14 fail. Conclusion: this is the first multi-row evidence that native paired left/right outputs can sometimes respond to action/state rather than pure visual context, but it is not yet reliable enough to call the UMI world model solved; the failure is not only bad row selection. Next step should keep the h768/scale0.05 recipe as the mainline seed, add explicit state/action consistency or inverse-dynamics auxiliary supervision, train/evaluate on temporally separated future windows with visible action outcomes, and preserve the 20-case strict gate as the acceptance test. Local reports with real synced videos: ~/Downloads/umi_paired_outres_gate_20260621/index.html and ~/Downloads/umi_paired_outres_highaction_gate_20260621/index.html.

Paired in-block branch-contrast gate 2026-06-21: the follow-up 8xH20 Wan2.2-A14B grid finished all high/low step-64 checkpoints and ran the same rows 0/1/5/7/11 strict action gate for four variants: preblock+output-head scale0.03, preblock+output-head scale0.05, preblock+output-residual h768, and block-control+output-head. Result is a clear negative: 0/20 strict passes, with actual vs zero/time_view_only/wrong-row MAE deltas mostly within ±0.1. This means branch-specific contrastive loss plus pre-block or block-control injection did not produce verifiable action sensitivity in the paired-output setting. Keep the earlier paired-output-residual h768/scale0.05 result as the current best multi-row seed (4/20 total, best recipe 2/5), but stop treating this in-block branch-contrast sweep as promising. Next route should change the supervision/interface rather than stacking more adapters: add an explicit state/action consistency decoder or inverse-dynamics auxiliary loss, train on temporally separated future windows where action changes visible outcomes, and keep the 20-case strict gate as the acceptance test. Local report with 160 real synced videos: ~/Downloads/umi_paired_inblock_branchcontrast_gate_20260621/index.html.

Global Image-Layered dataset v0 route locked 2026-06-21: method matrix across SAM3+inpaint / VACE masked-V2V / RoboTwin 2.0 synthetic / MaskWAM annotation loop / SAMVG-LIVE SVG sidecar (Qwen-Image-Layered + RevealLayer dropped as mainline per 06-17/18 group testing). CORE pipeline = MaskWAM-style Qwen-VL + SAM3 annotation loop -> per-entity matting -> VACE occluder/scene inpaint -> recomposition QA, with RoboTwin 2.0 per-object-ID masks/depth/pose as the free GT spine for decomposer training + QA threshold calibration. Open blockers: (1) audit RoboTwin grayscale masks for per-object IDs; (2) measure VACE clips/GPU-hour to decide VACE-everywhere vs SAM3+image-inpaint default. Comparison memo + full v0 plan (method matrix / IO schema / speed-cost / human-annotation / temporal consistency / recomposition QA / FoundationPose-IDM-reward utility / failure list / next-batch) backed up under weekly-briefs/memos/. Reference data-processing loop = MaskWAM annotation-pipeline figure (maskwam-annotation-pipeline-20260621.png). Next batch order: RoboTwin synthetic GT spine -> UMI das-data 3 demo clips (align with Gamma-World inference report) -> UMI 68 + Hoi! cross-view -> Gamma-World-generated videos.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / Stage 1 build-upon route. Gamma-World defines the synchronized action-conditioned interactive world-model lane, with Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and causal real-time rollout. Use it as the Stage 1 reference for UMI Ego World generation and shared-context multi-view rollout; then make the UMI contribution about robot-data utility, view/layer/eval contracts, IDM/control decoding, and policy ablations rather than agent-count scaling.

references.title: COMBO: Compositional World Models for Embodied Multi-Agent Cooperation
references.notes: Stage 1 shared-context reference. COMBO estimates overall world state from partial egocentric RGBD observations, factorizes multi-agent joint actions into compositional action conditions, and uses the resulting world model with VLM-assisted action proposal / intent tracking / outcome evaluation for online cooperation. Use it to frame UMI left/right/head views as partial observations of one latent scene, not independent video targets.

references.title: Solaris: Building a Multiplayer Video World Model in Minecraft
references.notes: Stage 1 multi-view consistency reference. Solaris builds a synchronized multiplayer video+action data system, trains through staged single-player to multiplayer modeling with causal and Self Forcing variants, introduces Checkpointed Self Forcing for longer-horizon supervision, and evaluates movement, memory, grounding, building, and view consistency. Use it as the strongest analogy for UMI's multiple generated camera streams sharing the same world state.

references.title: Dashboard asset: world-model taxonomy
references.notes: User-provided taxonomy figure. Keep as a visual guardrail for Tri-View: reconstruction models, next-step predictive models, and runnable simulators are different world-model claims and need different evaluation evidence.

references.title: Qwen-Image-Layered: Layered Decomposition for Inherent Editability
references.notes: NEGATIVE BASELINE / passed over for current robot-layer pipeline. Group tests on 2026-06-17/18 showed Qwen-Image-Layered can control layer count but cannot bind required robot/object/background semantics to each layer; prompt constraints act like captions, producing merged entities, residual/shadow/support layers, failed pure-background rendering, and weak inpainting. Keep as evidence that generic editable layers are insufficient for robot manipulation.

references.title: RevealLayer: Disentangling Hidden and Visible Layers via Occlusion-Aware Image Decomposition
references.notes: REFERENCE ONLY / not the short-term practical route after group testing. RevealLayer's occlusion-aware RGBA formulation remains useful background, but the current robot-scene probe was judged unusable. Do not depend on RevealLayer as the Stage 2 mainline unless new robot-specific guidance or code evidence changes this result.

references.title: ObjectForesight: Predicting Future 3D Object Trajectories from Human Videos
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / object-centric dynamics route. ObjectForesight predicts future 6-DoF object trajectories from short egocentric video histories and builds large-scale pseudo-ground-truth object trajectory data from human videos using segmentation, tracking, reconstruction, depth/camera motion, and pose estimation. Use as the reference for object/contact layer targets and for the segment+track+reconstruct data pipeline.

references.title: Walk through Paintings: Egocentric World Models from Internet Priors
references.notes: Support reference for Ego World: transforms pretrained video diffusion models into action-conditioned egocentric world models with motor-command conditioning and structural consistency evaluation.

references.title: LOME: Learning Human-Object Manipulation with Action-Conditioned Egocentric World Model
references.notes: Support reference for action-conditioned egocentric human-object interaction video: uses reference image, text prompt, and per-frame actions to generate physically plausible manipulation videos.

references.title: EgoSim: Egocentric World Simulator for Embodied Interaction Generation
references.notes: Support reference for continuously updated egocentric interaction simulation with 3D state updating, geometry-action-aware observation simulation, and scalable egocentric video data construction.

references.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
references.notes: Support reference for IDM: generated video rollouts can be decoded into actions by an inverse dynamics model, and IDM-derived rewards can align video generation with executable robot constraints.

references.title: RoboEngine: Plug-and-Play Robot Data Augmentation
references.notes: Background-layer / scene-adaptation reference. RoboEngine uses semantic robot segmentation and background generation for physics- and task-aware robot data augmentation, matching the scene-layer domain-randomization track.

references.title: Single-View Robot Pose and Joint Angle Estimation via Render & Compare
references.notes: Support reference for visible-robot branch: RoboPose estimates robot joint angles and 6D camera-to-robot pose from a single RGB image of a known articulated robot.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Support reference for no-arm-visible branch: visual/visual-inertial SLAM supports monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye cameras, suitable as a VO/SLAM baseline for wrist/head ego views.

references.title: VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer
references.notes: Support reference for feed-forward visual geometry: predicts camera parameters, depth, point maps, and 3D point tracks from one or many views, useful for wrist/head egomotion and object-scene geometry.

references.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
references.notes: Reference for efficient minute-scale world modeling, hybrid linear attention, precise 6-DoF camera control, and metric pose-supervised long-video generation.

references.title: iWorld-Bench: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
references.notes: Reference for evaluating interactive/camera-controllable world models with packaged metadata, camera trajectories, action-control, memory-ability, camera-following, and all-metric evaluation wrappers.

references.title: EgoX: Egocentric Video Generation from a Single Exocentric Video
references.notes: Exo-to-ego reference: point-cloud rendered ego prior, width/channel latent concatenation, geometry-guided self-attention, LoRA on Wan 2.1 14B; useful for cross-view conditioning design.

references.title: Intention-driven Ego-to-Exo Video Generation
references.notes: Ego-to-exo reference: uses view-independent intention, head trajectory, action description, optical flow and occlusion maps to synthesize exocentric video from egocentric input.

references.title: WorldWander: Bridging Egocentric and Exocentric Worlds in Video Generation
references.notes: Bidirectional ego/exo translation reference with in-context perspective alignment and synchronized ego-exo triplets; relevant for tri-view cross-view synchronization.

references.title: MolmoAct: Action Reasoning Models that can Reason in Space
references.notes: Dataset-design reference for this project, not a direct substitute for our missing view: MolmoAct's real-robot dataset uses two side-mounted views and one wrist camera, converts episodes into side+wrist paired examples, and adds depth/trace/action-reasoning supervision.

references.title: MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM Dataset
references.notes: LeRobot-format schema reference for synchronized bimanual manipulation data: camera streams are observation.images.top / left / right at 360x640 30fps, with 14D bimanual state/action. Useful for dataset packaging and ablations, but it does not resolve the current UMI/Jianzhi missing fixed robot-ego view.

references.title: Hoi!: A Multimodal Dataset for Force-Grounded, Cross-View Articulated Manipulation
references.notes: Selected dataset replacement substrate for UMI Image Layered World Model on 2026-06-17. Public Hoi! bundles cover synchronized head-ego, manipulator-centric ego / UMI-style, wrist, and fixed external views, making it the best available open data surface for shared-context multi ego-view manifests.

references.title: Dr. Robot: Differentiable Robot Rendering
references.notes: Robot-layer / IDM reference. Dr. Robot makes robot appearance differentiable with respect to control parameters, so it is a natural reference for whole-arm pose/joint recovery and for replacing a generic IDM with robot-kinematic visual gradients.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Wrist/head ego geometry baseline. ORB-SLAM3 supports visual and visual-inertial SLAM with monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye setups, which matches the need to recover wrist-camera motion when robot pixels are incomplete or absent.

references.title: LIV: Language-Image Representations and Rewards for Robotic Control
references.notes: Object-layer reward reference. LIV pretrains control-centric visual-language representations from human video without action labels and can produce language-conditioned rewards on human and robot videos, which maps to object/contact process reward.

references.title: ARRO: Augmented Reality for Robots
references.notes: Policy-utility evidence. ARRO shows that segmentation-driven augmented robot observations can improve Diffusion Policy and generalist robot policies; this supports the stronger ablation that full Image-Layered Robot Data should help manipulation.

references.title: 10Kh RealOmni-Open DataSet
references.notes: New Stage 1 data source from the UMI model-structure update. GenRobot describes 10Kh RealOmni-Open as real-world, diverse-skill embodied data with 1M+ data clips and ongoing expansion; use it for the dual-wrist Wan2.1 baseline only after checking view/camera availability, download access, license, task coverage, and annotation fields.

references.title: Wan2.2 MoE high-noise / low-noise architecture
references.notes: Training-risk reference for UMI: Wan2.2-A14B uses a two-expert denoising MoE, with a high-noise expert for early/layout steps and a low-noise expert for later/detail steps. This improves quality but complicates custom finetuning because UMI adaptation must decide whether and how to train each expert and where to switch by SNR/timestep.

references.title: Wan2.1 LoRA fine-tuning training guide
references.notes: Implementation reference for the first UMI training route: provides a complete Wan2.1 LoRA workflow covering dataset structure, metadata.json, model download, DeepSpeed/FSDP training, validation, and inference testing.

references.title: Finetrainers Wan training support
references.notes: Implementation reference: finetrainers documents LoRA and full-finetune support and lists tested Wan2.1 T2V/I2V/FLF2V checkpoints. Use as a cleaner baseline toolchain candidate before modifying dual-wrist inputs.

references.title: VACE: All-in-One Video Creation and Editing
references.notes: Video inpainting / masked V2V candidate for Stage 2. Official VACE repo describes reference-to-video, video-to-video editing, masked video-to-video editing, and free composition, making it a direct fit for mask-tracking -> video inpaint -> recomposition QA.

references.title: Wan2.1 VACE model release
references.notes: Wan2.1 VACE release reference. Use for inference/code/weights alignment when testing VACE on robot-mask clips; keep Wan2.1 compatibility with the Stage 1 training route.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0: scalable dual-arm manipulation data generator and benchmark
references.notes: Candidate mask-rich synthetic data source for Stage 2. RoboTwin 2.0 is built for bimanual manipulation data generation and benchmarking, with SAPIEN-based simulation, strong domain randomization, and a 50-task / 731-object benchmark surface. Group note: inspect whether provided grayscale masks expose per-object mask IDs directly usable for layer pseudo-labels.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0 official repository
references.notes: Implementation/data-audit target for checking mask files, grayscale ID conventions, camera views, object metadata, and whether generated episodes can export robot/object/contact/occluder/background masks.

references.title: MaskWAM: Unifying Mask Prompting and Prediction for World-Action Models
references.notes: Comparison paper for the mask-centric WAM direction. MaskWAM integrates masks as explicit inputs and future predictions via a unified Mixture of Transformers, addressing spatial ambiguity and semantic grounding. For UMI, borrow the annotation loop and future-mask-prediction lesson, but keep the contribution as reusable robot/object-contact/occluder/scene RGBA/video layers with inpaint, SVG/contact sidecars, recomposition QA, and IDM/reward/FoundationPose utility.

references.title: MaskWAM annotation pipeline figure
references.notes: Dashboard copy of the MaskWAM data-processing figure: language annotation and VLM object extraction initialize SAM3 mask sequences, human point prompts correct ambiguous rounds, and accepted masks become labeled episodes. Use as a practical reference for the global layered dataset annotation loop.

references.title: MaskWAM vs UMI memo + v0 dataset plan (private backup)
references.notes: Moved to PRIVATE BorisGuo6/Gamma-World repo (robot-reproduction branch, docs/research/): MaskWAM-vs-UMI comparison memo + global Image-Layered dataset v0 plan (method matrix / IO schema / cost / QA / utility / failures / next-batch). Not published on the public site. Core distinction: MaskWAM=mask-as-policy-supervision; UMI=layers-as-reusable-data-infrastructure.

risks_decisions: Architecture update 2026-06-10: keep one main research card, but organize execution as three sub-projects: UMI Ego World robot-data generation, Robot Video Image-Layered Model, and low-level layered-data utility for IDM/reward/adaptation.

Core claim: prove Image-Layered Robot Data helps robot manipulation. Clean decomposition is necessary but not sufficient; the evidence must be downstream policy, IDM, reward/eval, or adaptation improvement.
Robot layer is not only a mask. It should carry enough temporal and kinematic signal to support pose estimation, gripper/arm state estimation, inverse dynamics, or wrist-camera egomotion estimation.
Object layer is the reward/process layer. It should preserve object identity, contact, 6-DoF motion, and task-progress information rather than only foreground pixels.

Background layer is an adaptation handle. Randomizing or generating backgrounds must not corrupt robot/object interaction labels, and evaluation should separate visual robustness from dynamics hallucination.

Dataset risk: pseudo-layers from generic image-layer agents may be semantically wrong and temporally unstable. The v0 needs recomposition error, temporal consistency, object-contact preservation, and IDM recoverability metrics before large-scale training.

Qwen/RevealLayer decision 2026-06-18: pass/drop both as short-term direct robot-layer generators. Qwen fails semantic layer binding despite layer-count control, and the RevealLayer/ICML-style result was judged unusable on the current robot-bed scene. Keep them as references/negative baselines while the practical route moves to SAM3/SAM mask tracking + inpaint/VACE + SVG sidecars.

Stage 1 shared-context decision 2026-06-19: treat Gamma-World, COMBO, and Solaris as positive references for synchronized multi-agent / multi-view world modeling. The UMI claim should be narrower and testable: generate multiple robot-camera videos that share overlapping scene/object/contact context under action conditioning, then prove those outputs and their layers improve IDM, reward/eval, FoundationPose, or policy training.
Ablation requirement: compare flat RGB, mask-only/SAM, and full layered data under the same policy and reward/eval setup. Without this ablation, the claim stays qualitative.

Compute decision 2026-06-17: AutoDL is capped at single-node 8-GPU training for this effort; no cross-machine distributed training. Scale plans, expected batch size, checkpointing/offload, and model-choice decisions must stay inside that envelope until a separate multi-node cluster is provisioned.

Coordination decision 2026-06-17: do not wait for a perfect end-to-end layer generator before proving utility. Use the best available manually assisted or semi-automatic layered clips to test FoundationPose, IDM, reward/eval, and recomposition first; only then scale toward a global layered dataset.

Architecture decision 2026-06-19: after paired output-head validation, action/state token injection alone is no longer the mainline. It produces a measurable but modest gap against zero/time/wrong-row controls. The next Wan2.2-A14B implementation should prioritize zero-init residual or native in-block control branches with branch-specific action/state injection and explicit output-head losses.

MaskWAM dataset decision 2026-06-21: borrow MaskWAM's VLM -> SAM3 -> human-verification mask propagation loop and its future-mask-prediction ablation as evidence, but do not collapse UMI into a mask-only WAM. The global dataset route should persist raster mask/RGBA/video truth plus SVG/contact metadata and should test full layered data against flat RGB and mask-only baselines.

Action-conditioning gate result 2026-06-21: implemented and ran Wan2.2-A14B paired output-residual + action/state consistency auxiliary-head grid on 8 H20 GPUs. Four weights (0.05/0.1/0.2/0.5) trained to step-64 for both high/low experts and were evaluated on rows 0/1/5/7/11 against zero, time/view-only, and wrong-row controls. Strict gate result: 2/20 pass overall; best weights only reached 1/5 each (ac0.1 row1, ac0.2 row0). Conclusion: the auxiliary head does not yet make the model reliably action-conditioned; keep the report as negative evidence and move next to a stronger native control branch / ControlNet-style residual path with explicit action-state decoding and branch-specific losses.

timeline.target_note: Paper target for the tri-view layered manipulation world model; schedule should be updated once the exact submission plan is fixed.

milestones.title: Merge Image-Layered WM into UMI tri-view layered world-model card
Tri-view data schema and layer/eval ablation spec ready
